# Atlas Research Institute

> A free, four-week research fellowship for high school students who study education inequality in their own region using real datasets (UNESCO, World Bank, PISA) and produce published work. Applicants come from 15+ countries.

Atlas Research Institute is a youth-led nonprofit addressing global education inequality. Fellows study educational disparity in their own region using established datasets and turn their analysis into a policy brief or literature review intended for publication. The program is free and remote. No prior research experience is required.

## What Atlas Does

- Runs a free, four-week research fellowship for high school students (grades 9-12)
- Trains fellows in survey and policy research methodology through live seminars with researchers from institutions like Stanford
- Guides fellows to analyze education inequality in their own region using UNESCO, World Bank, and PISA data
- Supports fellows toward publication in the Atlas Journal of Education Policy and partner journals
- Provides scholarships to fellows and research leads through a partnership with Lumiere Education

## Who It Is For

- High school students (grades 9-12) interested in research, policy, or social science
- Students seeking a mentored research experience and published research credentials
- No prior research experience required
- 100% free, remote, open to students worldwide

## Reach

Applications have come from students in 15+ countries, across both well-resourced and under-resourced education systems, including Bangladesh, Bolivia, Canada, China, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Nepal, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Taiwan, Thailand, UAE, and the USA.

## How It Works

1. Students apply with three short essays. No resume required.
2. Selected fellows train in research methodology through live seminars.
3. Fellows analyze education inequality in their own region using real datasets.
4. Fellows write a policy brief or literature review with editorial support.
5. Strong work is submitted for publication in the Atlas Journal of Education Policy and partner journals.

## Key Details

- Website: https://atlas-research.org
- Apply: https://atlas-research.org/apply.html
- Applications: open now, due July 24, 2026
- Cohort: four weeks, starting August 3, 2026
- Cost: free
- Focus: education access and inequality, policy research
- Publishing partners: International Journal of High School Research (IJHSR), The Curieux Review
- Program partner: Lumiere Education (scholarships for fellows and research leads)

## Related Searches
